scenes.speaker: {{playerName}}
scenes.text: Today is my first day at Akebono Brake Astra! I'm so excited to join the team.

scenes.speaker: AKE-BOT
scenes.text: Welcome aboard, {{playerName}}! I'm AKE-BOT, your cybersecurity guide here at Akebono. My mission is to keep you safe in the digital world!

scenes.speaker: AKE-BOT
scenes.text: The cyber threat landscape is evolving fast. Our systems are constantly targeted by hackers. One of the most dangerous is a hacker known as Ph1sh!

scenes.speaker: Ph1sh (Unknown)
scenes.text: Hehehe... A new employee? Fresh meat for my phishing attacks... They won't know what hit them. 😈

scenes.speaker: {{playerName}}
scenes.text: Let me check my work email. There's already a message waiting for me...

email.subject: URGENT: Your account will be suspended in 24 hours!
email.body:
Dear Employee,

Your corporate account has been flagged for suspicious activity and will be SUSPENDED within 24 hours unless you verify your identity.

Click here immediately: http://akebono-verify.xyz/login

Enter your username and password to avoid account suspension.

IT Security Team
Akebono Brake Corp.
redFlags: Asks for credentials via email (IT never does this!)

scenes.speaker: AKE-BOT
scenes.text: ⚠️ Careful! You just received this email. What do you do?

scenes.question: You receive an urgent email saying your account will be suspended. What do you do?

choices.text: 🖱️ Click the link and log in immediately
choices.consequence: You clicked a phishing link and entered your credentials! Ph1sh now has your login details and can access company systems.
choices.lesson: NEVER click suspicious links in emails, even if they look urgent. Phishers use urgency to bypass your critical thinking. Always verify with IT directly.

choices.text: 📧 Forward the email to all colleagues
choices.consequence: Forwarding the phishing email spread it to all your colleagues, putting the entire company at risk!
choices.lesson: Don't forward suspicious emails. This spreads the attack. Instead, report it to IT Security.

choices.text: 🗑️ Delete the email without reporting it
choices.consequence: By deleting without reporting, other employees may still receive and fall for this phishing attack.
choices.lesson: Always report phishing emails — don't just delete them. IT Security needs to block the sender and warn others.

choices.text: 🚨 Report it to IT Security and don't click anything

scenes.speaker: AKE-BOT
scenes.text: Excellent work! 🎉 You identified the threat and reported it correctly! Let me show you the red flags in that email...

scenes.title: 📚 Phishing Red Flags
points: Wrong sender domain: akeb0n0-brake.com (zeros, not letter O!)
Suspicious URL: akebono-verify.xyz instead of official domain
Creates artificial urgency: '24 hours!' to stop your thinking
Asks for credentials — IT NEVER asks for your password via email
Poor grammar/formatting is a common phishing sign

scenes.question: IT Security confirmed it was a phishing email. They ask you to check for more suspicious emails. You find one from 'CEO@akebono.com' asking for a wire transfer. What do you do?

choices.text: 💸 Process the transfer — the CEO is asking!
choices.consequence: You processed a fraudulent wire transfer! This is Business Email Compromise (BEC) — a very common and costly attack.
choices.lesson: Always verify financial requests through another channel (phone call) before acting. No matter who the email appears to be from.

choices.text: 📞 Call the CEO directly to verify the request
📧 Reply to the email asking for more details
choices.consequence: Replying confirms your email is active and you're susceptible. The attacker will send more convincing requests.
choices.lesson: Never reply to suspicious emails. Always verify through an independent, trusted channel.

choices.text: 🚨 Forward to IT Security for investigation

scenes.speaker: AKE-BOT
scenes.text: Perfect! You verified through an independent channel. The CEO confirmed they never sent that email — it was Ph1sh using Business Email Compromise!

scenes.speaker: Ph1sh
scenes.text: Curse you! My phishing attack failed because of you! But this isn't over, rookie... I have more tricks up my sleeve! 😠

scenes.title: 🎉 Great Job! — Good Ending
scenes.message: You successfully identified and reported both phishing attempts, protecting Akebono from a serious data breach! Ph1sh's first attack has been foiled.

scenes.title: 💥 Security Breach — Bad Ending
scenes.message: You fell for Ph1sh's phishing attack! Your credentials were stolen, potentially compromising the entire company's systems. Don't worry — this is a learning moment!
lesson_recap: Always verify email sender domains carefully
Never click links in urgent, threatening emails
Report suspicious emails to IT Security immediately
IT will never ask for your password via email

scenes.title: 💸 BEC Attack Succeeded — Bad Ending
scenes.message: Ph1sh used Business Email Compromise to trick you. Always verify unusual requests through a separate, trusted communication channel.
lesson_recap: Verify financial/sensitive requests via phone or in person
Even emails from the CEO can be spoofed
Never process unusual requests from email alone
Report suspicious emails before acting on them
